summary: Created the US-845 desktop table states QA design document at docs/test-design/us-845-desktop-table-states.md. The document defines executable desktop/laptop UI scenarios for bidding, contract ordering, widow pickup, discard, whist/check decisions, contract play, all-pass, misere, deal settlement, next-deal, and final-result states; includes active-actor, legal-action, contract/trump, trick/hands, and score-table verification expectations; traces REQ-1762, AC-2428, and AC-2429; and routes downstream work to MZ-502, MZ-503, accessibility verification, visual regression, component regression, and the release gate. The design explicitly depends on accepted US-842 and US-844 evidence and stays within the existing Vitest/jsdom plus Playwright-style screenshot workflow.

tests_run.evidence: Verified the US-845 QA design document exists in the worktree and is readable as plain text.

verification_environment.verification_scope: docs-only QA design update with targeted source/report inspection and document readability verification

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop table states must show current phase, active actor, legal human actions, contract, trump when applicable, current trick, player hands, score table, deal result, and final result.
evidence_matrix.limitations: This task defines executable scenarios and routing only; downstream implementation and verification tasks must provide runtime evidence.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop/laptop states for bidding, contract, whist, play, deal-result, and final-result remain understandable and do not block normal play.
evidence_matrix.limitations: The task records proposed executable checks; it does not itself prove final UI behavior.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Deal-result and next-deal states must make the result understandable and let the player proceed unless the bullet is complete.
evidence_matrix.limitations: This row captures design traceability and downstream test targets, not fresh runtime verification.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop UI scenarios inherit legal phase and legal-action expectations from the accepted US-842 rule-phase design and release gate.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Legal-action behavior is assumed from accepted upstream evidence and must remain aligned in downstream implementation.

evidence_matrix.scenario: Desktop UI scenarios inherit accepted score/result/final-result evidence boundaries and screenshot classification from US-844 artifacts.
evidence_matrix.limitations: Visual artifact classification is reused as precedent; no new screenshots were required for this docs-only task.

quality_gate_matrix.limitations: This task defines accessibility expectations and ownership only; downstream verification must supply runtime evidence.
The report routes required screenshot review but does not include fresh captures for this docs-only task.
This row identifies the required regression surfaces and ownership, not completed regression execution.
quality_gate_matrix.downstream_owner: MZ-503 integration tests and component regression

quality_gate_matrix.limitations: Release-gate evidence will be produced by later verifier tasks after implementation and regression complete.
This task assigns implementation expectations only; it does not perform presentation-client changes.
Security review is out of scope because this task only defines local desktop UI QA scenarios and changes no production code.
